cases.title: Scenario matrix row for majority class completion within 5 minutes of session open
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.1 performance tests and §11 NFR verification matrix rows for NFR-02 and AC-21 apply.
Representative enrolled class sections are seeded with pilot-scale enrollment counts per product-meta mvpScale.
Class sessions are in Scheduled state with active enrollments and lecturer assigned to each section.
steps: Open attendance for each representative section via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open (AC-01).
Record openedAt as T=0 for the 5-minute completion window.
Simulate enrolled students submitting valid POST /v1/check-ins until T+5 minutes.
For each section, compute completion rate: count of students with successful Present or Late attendance divided by total enrolled students.
cases.expected: For each representative enrolled class section, more than 50% of enrolled students complete successful check-in within 5 minutes of openedAt, satisfying AC-21 and NFR-02 per testing-plan §7.1 class-start burst target.

cases.title: Attendance ledger and enrollment modules compute section completion rate within 5-minute window
preconditions: Real database transaction boundary is enabled for attendance ledger, enrollment, and class-session modules.
Class section has known enrollment count (e.g. 40 students) with no prior attendance for the target session.
Session openedAt timestamp is persisted and queryable from ClassSession record.
steps: Lecturer opens session via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open; capture openedAt.
Submit successful check-ins for a majority subset of enrolled students (e.g. 25 of 40) with checkInAt timestamps before openedAt + 5 minutes.
Query attendance ledger and enrollment tables to compute completedCount / enrolledCount within the window.
Verify checkInAt for each success record falls between openedAt and openedAt + 5 minutes.
cases.expected: Module integration correctly persists AttendanceRecord rows with Present or Late status; computed completion rate exceeds 50% within the 5-minute window from openedAt, matching AC-21 majority gate and NFR-02 classroom completion time.

cases.title: Session open transition starts 5-minute completion clock at openedAt
preconditions: Class session is in Scheduled state with enrolled students and assigned lecturer.
Lecturer bearer token is available for POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
No attendance records exist for the session before open.
steps: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer with Idempotency-Key.
Verify response HTTP 200 with data.state Open and data.openedAt ISO-8601 timestamp.
Confirm session state in database transitions Scheduled to Open with openedAt and actor recorded.
Use openedAt as T=0 baseline for subsequent completion-rate measurement in the 5-minute window.
cases.expected: Session transitions to Open with openedAt recorded (AC-01); the 5-minute majority-completion measurement window begins at openedAt per AC-21 Given/When/Then and WF-03 SLO alignment (NFR-02).

cases.title: Concurrent class-start burst achieves majority completion with duplicate prevention intact
preconditions: Open class session with 30+ enrolled students and valid rotating QR tokens.
Test harness fires parallel POST /v1/check-ins from distinct student actors with unique Idempotency-Key values.
Each student has no prior successful attendance for the session.
steps: At T+0 to T+3 minutes after session open, trigger parallel check-in submissions across the enrolled cohort.
Include replay attempts: same student resubmits with same Idempotency-Key and a second distinct submission after success (AC-08).
After burst completes, query attendance records and compute completion rate within openedAt + 5 minutes.
Verify duplicate submissions return 409 DuplicateCheckIn without inflating completion count.
cases.expected: Majority of enrolled students achieve successful check-in within 5 minutes under concurrent burst (NFR-04); duplicate prevention rejects second success per student (AC-08, BR-07) so completion rate reflects unique successful records only.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance reflects majority completion within 5 minutes
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session; majority of enrolled students have completed POST /v1/check-ins within 5 minutes of openedAt.
Authenticated Lecturer bearer token is available for roster query.
E2E harness can poll attendance endpoint at T+4 minutes and T+5 minutes.
steps: At T+4 minutes after open, GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as assigned Lecturer.
Verify HTTP 200, standard envelope, data.counts.present + data.counts.late divided by enrolled total exceeds 50%.
At T+5 minutes, repeat GET and confirm counts.data.rows reflect Present or Late for majority of enrolled students.
Verify data.state is Open and pagination metadata does not leak cross-scope counts.
cases.expected: HTTP 200 roster response shows majority of enrolled students with Present or Late status within 5 minutes of session open; counts align with AC-21 completion gate and FR-19 live roster contract.

cases.title: WF-03 class-start flow achieves majority student completion within 5-minute SLO
preconditions: Representative enrolled class section with pilot-scale student count is configured.
Lecturer actor can open session and display rotating QR (WF-02, AC-01).
Student actors are enrolled, authenticated, and eligible for check-in (AC-06, AC-07).
steps: Lecturer opens attendance session (POST /open); record openedAt as T=0.
Enrolled students execute WF-03 happy path: scan QR, authenticate if needed, submit POST /v1/check-ins.
Stagger submissions realistically across T+0 to T+4 minutes mimicking class-start adoption curve.
At T+5 minutes, query roster and compute (present + late) / enrolled.
cases.expected: Primary WF-03 flow yields majority (>50%) of enrolled students with successful Present or Late attendance within 5 minutes of open, matching AC-21, NFR-02, and workflow observability target in 06-main-workflows.md §11.1.

cases.title: Majority completion gate fails when fewer than 50% check in within 5 minutes
preconditions: Performance test environment can throttle check-in throughput or limit student participation for threshold validation.
Class section has known enrollment count; session is open with openedAt recorded.
Release gate documentation requires explicit pass/fail evidence for AC-21.
steps: Open session and allow only a minority subset (e.g. 40%) of enrolled students to complete check-in before openedAt + 5 minutes.
At T+5 minutes, query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and compute completion rate.
Compare rate against AC-21 majority (>50%) threshold and document gate verdict.
Optionally run control scenario where >50% complete to confirm PASS boundary.
cases.expected: When computed completion rate is <= 50% at the 5-minute mark, AC-21 gate is marked FAIL with documented metric evidence; when rate > 50% under normal pilot profile, gate is PASS. Boundary validates measurability of the 5-minute majority threshold per NFR-02.

cases.title: Non-Student roles denied POST /v1/check-ins so only enrolled students count toward completion
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken and known enrolled student count.
Lecturer and DepartmentAdmin bearer tokens are available per permission matrix.
Completion metric baseline is enrolled student count only.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and Authorization header.
DepartmentAdmin calls POST /v1/check-ins with the same qrToken context.
Verify both receive HTTP 403 Forbidden per permission matrix (Submit QR check-in Deny).
Confirm enrolled students can still complete check-ins and majority completion metric excludes non-student attempts.
cases.expected: Lecturer and DepartmentAdmin receive 403 Forbidden; no CheckInAttempt or AttendanceRecord is created for non-student actors. Only authenticated enrolled Student submissions contribute to the AC-21 majority completion rate within 5 minutes.

cases.title: Lecturer realtime roster UI shows majority checked-in badge within 5 minutes of open
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated on desktop Chromium viewport with assigned open session.
Enrolled student cohort will complete mobile-web check-in journeys within the 5-minute window.
Browser harness records wall-clock from session open UI action through majority roster indicator.
steps: Lecturer navigates to session control and opens attendance (AC-01); confirm Open state and QR display.
Multiple enrolled students complete FLOW-01 mobile-web check-in journeys in parallel (emulated mobile viewports).
Observe lecturer realtime roster dashboard: present + late counts and progress indicator update without page reload.
At T+5 minutes, verify UI shows majority (>50%) of enrolled students as checked in (Present or Late).
cases.expected: Lecturer dashboard reflects majority completion within 5 minutes of session open without manual refresh; present/late counts and visual progress align with AC-21 gate and NFR-02 classroom completion SLO.

cases.title: Pilot campus class-start completion timing on physical mobile browsers
preconditions: Staging pilot uses real iOS Safari or Android Chrome on campus Wi-Fi per testing-plan §6.2 browser matrix.
Representative class section with enrolled students; lecturer displays classroom QR.
Physical devices and campus network are not reproducible in Playwright MCP harness.
steps: Lecturer opens session in classroom; record open time as T=0.
Enrolled students scan QR and complete check-in on physical mobile devices under normal campus network.
At T+5 minutes, lecturer verifies roster shows majority checked in via mobile or desktop view.
Aggregate completion rate across pilot sessions for release evidence.
cases.expected: Physical pilot demonstrates majority of enrolled students complete check-in within 5 minutes under real campus conditions, providing production-like evidence for AC-21 beyond emulated harness runs.
